wines.name: Royal Tokaji The Oddity Dry Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Aged in older Hungarian oak barrels

pairings.why: The wine's racy acidity and citrus notes complement the delicate fish and cut through the butter.
Mineral salinity mirrors the brininess of shellfish; the acidity refreshes the palate.
Tangy chèvre finds an echo in the wine's citrus-mineral register.

history.summary: Launched under Royal Tokaji's modern dry-Furmint range to demonstrate the grape's potential beyond sweet Aszú. The name reflects the estate's view that dry Furmint from Mád was still an unusual choice when first bottled.

milestones.event: First vintage of The Oddity released
Wine adopted stainless-steel and old barrel maturation to emphasise mineral purity

wines.description: Dry Furmint from Royal Tokaji's Mád estate, showing the grape's mineral, citrus-driven character without any residual sweetness.

wines.name: Royal Tokaji Mézes Mály Aszú 6 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in small Hungarian oak barrels (gönci) for 18-24 months

pairings.why: The wine's acidity cuts through the richness of foie gras while matching its luxurious texture.
Blue cheese salt and funk find a counterpoint in the Aszú's dried-fruit sweetness.
Stone-fruit desserts echo the Aszú's apricot and marmalade character.

history.summary: The Mézes Mály vineyard (the name means 'honeyed slope') in Tarcal is classified first-class in the 1700 Rákóczi classification. Royal Tokaji vinified Mézes Mály separately from the estate's founding in 1990.

milestones.event: Royal Tokaji founded; Mézes Mály identified as a premier single-vineyard site
First Mézes Mály single-vineyard Aszú 6 puttonyos released
Mézes Mály established as flagship single-vineyard label of the estate

wines.description: Single-vineyard 6-puttonyos Aszú from the first-class Mézes Mály dűlő in Tarcal, the Royal Tokaji estate's flagship sweet expression.

wines.name: Royal Tokaji Nyulászó Aszú 6 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in gönci oak barrels for 18-24 months

pairings.why: Honey and floral notes bridge into the cheese's savoury complexity.
Custard richness and caramel echo the wine's butterscotch and dried-fruit notes.
The wine's acidity cuts the fat while the sweetness complements the richness.

history.summary: Nyulászó (the name means 'hunting warren') in Mád is classified first-class in the historical Rákóczi classification. Royal Tokaji has vinified it separately since the estate's first commercial vintages.

milestones.event: First Nyulászó single-vineyard Aszú bottling from Royal Tokaji
Nyulászó confirmed as one of the estate's three premier single-vineyard labels

wines.description: Single-vineyard 6-puttonyos Aszú from the first-class Nyulászó dűlő in Mád, one of Royal Tokaji's flagship sweet wines.

wines.name: Royal Tokaji Szt. Tamás Aszú 6 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in gönci oak for 18-24 months

pairings.why: Nuttiness and spice in the wine echo the roasted pumpkin; acidity lightens the cream.
Marzipan and toasted hazelnut notes in the wine align with the almond filling.
Washed-rind cheese pungency is tempered by the wine's sweetness and fruit lift.

history.summary: Szt. Tamás (St. Thomas) is one of Mád's three first-class classified vineyards, the others being Nyulászó and Király. Royal Tokaji has bottled it as a single-vineyard Aszú since the estate's earliest commercial vintages.

milestones.event: First Szt. Tamás single-vineyard Aszú release
Szt. Tamás identified as the estate's consistently spice-forward dűlő expression

wines.description: Single-vineyard 6-puttonyos Aszú from the first-class Szt. Tamás dűlő in Mád, one of Royal Tokaji's three historic single-vineyard sweet wines.

wines.name: Royal Tokaji Aszú 5 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in gönci oak barrels for 18 months

pairings.why: Peach and apricot notes in the wine mirror ripe stone-fruit fillings.

pairings.dish: Brie de Meaux with quince paste
pairings.why: Soft cheese and quince sweetness find a complementary match in the 5-puttonyos character.
Clean cream and vanilla are natural companions for this warmly sweet Aszú.

history.summary: Royal Tokaji's estate blend Aszú at 5 puttonyos has been produced since the first commercial vintage in 1990, representing the estate's multi-vineyard interpretation of the style at a slightly lower sweetness level than the flagship 6-puttonyos single-vineyard labels.

milestones.event: First Royal Tokaji vintage; Aszú 5 puttonyos the accessible tier of the range
Hungarian Aszú regulations revised; 5-puttonyos designation standardised to minimum 120 g/l residual sugar

wines.description: Royal Tokaji's multi-vineyard estate Aszú 5 puttonyos, an approachable introduction to the estate's sweet wine range.

wines.name: Royal Tokaji Late Harvest
wines.oak_regime: Matured in older Hungarian oak

pairings.why: The wine's stone-fruit register echoes the pastry filling without overwhelming sweetness.
The lower-intensity sweetness of late harvest works with milder blue cheeses.
Subtle sweetness and gentle spice complement delicate pear desserts.

history.summary: The Late Harvest was added to Royal Tokaji's range to offer an entry-level sweet wine in years when botrytis levels are not sufficient for full Aszú production, and as a more accessible price point for the estate's sweet range.

milestones.event: First Royal Tokaji Late Harvest vintage

wines.description: Royal Tokaji's Late Harvest Furmint and Hárslevelű blend, an accessible introduction to the estate's sweet wine character.

wines.name: Royal Tokaji Betsek Aszú 6 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in gönci oak for 18-24 months

pairings.why: Tropical fruit notes in the wine echo the pavlova's exotic topping.
Caramel and vanilla notes bridge naturally into the custard.
Mild creamy blue cheese finds its match in the wine's richness and citrus lift.

history.summary: The Betsek dűlő in Mád was added to Royal Tokaji's single-vineyard Aszú lineup in the late 1990s as the estate expanded its identification and separate vinification of individual classified sites.

milestones.event: Betsek first bottled as a separate single-vineyard Aszú

wines.description: Single-vineyard 6-puttonyos Aszú from the Betsek dűlő in Mád, one of Royal Tokaji's classified single-site sweet wines.

wines.name: Disznókő Tokaji Aszú 6 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in small oak barrels for approximately 2 years

pairings.why: Classic pairing: Aszú's sweetness and acidity complement the richness of foie gras.
The sweet-sour tension of Aszú tames the intensity of strong blue cheese.
The floral and orange notes in the wine harmonise with the custard and caramel.

history.summary: Disznókő was acquired by AXA Millésimes in 1992 and the first commercial Aszú appeared from the 1993 vintage. The 104-hectare contiguous estate at Mezőzombor, one of the largest single-ownership plots in Tokaj, allows Disznókő to produce Aszú consistently in good vintages.

milestones.event: AXA Millésimes acquires Disznókő and begins full renovation
First commercial Aszú vintage under AXA ownership
Disznókő introduces estate concept with fully contiguous 104-hectare block

wines.description: Disznókő's flagship 6-puttonyos Aszú from the 104-hectare AXA Millésimes-owned estate at Mezőzombor, among the most consistent Tokaj Aszú expressions.

wines.name: Disznókő Tokaji Aszú 5 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in small oak barrels for 18 months

pairings.why: 5-puttonyos sweetness and citrus acidity balance the liver richness.
Apricot in both the wine and the jam create a harmonious pairing.
Stone-fruit in the wine amplifies the tart's caramelised peach filling.

history.summary: The 5-puttonyos Aszú has been part of Disznókő's range since the first commercial vintage, offering a slightly less concentrated but equally characteristic expression of the estate's botrytised production.

milestones.event: 5-puttonyos first released alongside the 6-puttonyos

wines.description: Disznókő's 5-puttonyos Aszú from the Mezőzombor estate; approachable concentration with vivid stone-fruit character.

wines.name: Disznókő Inspiráció Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Partial oak maturation in used French barrels

pairings.why: The wine's mineral salinity and citrus notes complement the sweet crayfish flesh.
Delicate white meat and caper acidity work with the wine's savoury mineral register.
Tangy soft cheese echoes the wine's citrus and mineral notes.

history.summary: Inspiráció was introduced as Disznókő's dry Furmint label to respond to growing international demand for the dry expression of Tokaj's main grape variety. The name ('inspiration') reflects the estate's desire to showcase Furmint's versatility beyond sweet wine.

milestones.event: Inspiráció Furmint first released as Disznókő's dry white label

wines.description: Disznókő's dry Furmint label, showcasing the Mezőzombor terroir's mineral salinity and racy acidity in an unoaked to lightly oaked style.

wines.name: Disznókő Édes Szamorodni
wines.oak_regime: Aged in gönci oak for 12-18 months

pairings.why: Hazelnut and caramel notes in the wine complement the walnut's earthy richness.
The Szamorodni's moderate sweetness and acidity handle softer blue cheeses well.
Caramelised apple in both the wine and pastry form an echo pairing.

history.summary: Szamorodni is a traditional Tokaj style predating modern Aszú; 'Szamorodni' means 'as it was born' in Polish, describing the practice of harvesting botrytised and healthy grapes together. Disznókő produces both sweet (édes) and dry (száraz) versions.

milestones.event: Édes Szamorodni introduced to Disznókő's range

wines.description: Disznókő's sweet Szamorodni, a traditional Tokaj style from whole-cluster botrytised harvest, with amber depth and hazelnut warmth.

wines.name: Disznókő Késői Szüretelésű
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel or older oak

pairings.why: Lychee and peach in the wine amplify the tropical fruit flavours.
Citrus acidity in the wine matches the cheesecake topping.
Light sweetness handles the mildest blue styles well.

history.summary: Disznókő's Késői Szüretelésű (Late Harvest) was introduced as the accessible tier of the estate's sweet range, vinified from late-picked but less botrytised grapes for a fresher style.

milestones.event: Késői Szüretelésű first released as Disznókő's entry-level sweet label

wines.description: Disznókő's entry-level late-harvest sweet wine; fresh and fruit-forward, an accessible introduction to the estate's sweet production.

wines.name: Disznókő Eszencia
wines.oak_regime: Slow fermentation and ageing in small oak; very low alcohol

pairings.why: The rarity and concentration of Eszencia makes solo contemplation the primary occasion.

pairings.dish: With a single square of 70% dark chocolate
pairings.why: Intense bittersweet chocolate is one of the few things not overwhelmed by Eszencia's concentration.

history.summary: Eszencia (Essence) is the rarest Tokaj category: only the gravity-drained free-run juice of individually selected botrytised Aszú berries, with residual sugar of 450-900 g/l. Disznókő produces it only in exceptional vintages. Historically it was considered medicinal and reserved for the Habsburg court.

milestones.event: Disznókő's first Eszencia vintage released commercially
Disznókő Eszencia produced in the outstanding 2007 vintage

wines.description: Disznókő Eszencia: the free-run juice of individually selected Aszú berries, produced only in exceptional vintages, with extraordinary sugar concentration.

wines.name: Oremus Mandolás Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Partial fermentation and ageing in Hungarian oak barrels

pairings.why: Almond notes in wine and garnish align; the mineral acidity pairs with freshwater fish.
The wine's citrus-almond register complements light white-meat preparations.
Mineral salinity cuts through the hollandaise while echoing asparagus's green notes.

history.summary: Mandolás (meaning 'almond grove') is one of the oldest recorded dűlő names in the Tolcsva valley and is the flagship dry Furmint of Oremus, acquired by Tempos Vega Sicilia in 1993. The vineyard's character was identified by the Vega Sicilia ownership as the estate's flagship dry-wine expression.

milestones.event: Vega Sicilia acquires Oremus; Mandolás identified as the flagship dry wine
Mandolás Furmint enters international distribution

wines.description: Oremus's flagship dry Furmint from the Mandolás vineyard in Tolcsva, a key single-vineyard expression for the dry Tokaj style.

verified.address_quoted: Bajcsy-Zsilinszky Endre utca 45., H-3934 Tolcsva, Hungary

wines.name: Oremus Tokaji Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel with minimal oak contact

pairings.why: The wine's fresh citrus and mineral character complement Hungary's traditional halászlé.
Tangy soft cheese and the wine's crisp acidity mirror each other.
Classic high-acid dry white and bivalve pairing; mineral salinity in both.

history.summary: Oremus's multi-vineyard estate Furmint has been produced since the first commercial vintage under Vega Sicilia ownership, offering an accessible introduction to the estate's dry wine style before the Mandolás single-vineyard tier.

milestones.event: First Tokaji Furmint produced under Vega Sicilia ownership

wines.description: Oremus's estate-level dry Furmint: vivid, mineral and fresh, an accessible introduction to the dry Tokaj style.

verified.address_quoted: Bajcsy-Zsilinszky Endre utca 45., H-3934 Tolcsva, Hungary

wines.name: Oremus Tokaji Aszú 5 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in oak barrels (gönci) for 18 months

pairings.why: Sweet-salt-fat balance is the Tokaji Aszú's classic domain.
Walnut and caramel notes in the wine echo the tart's savoury-sweet filling.

pairings.dish: Vanilla panna cotta with apricot compote
pairings.why: Apricot in the wine echoes the compote; vanilla cream softens the sweetness.

history.summary: Oremus's 5-puttonyos Aszú has been produced since the estate's first commercial vintage under Vega Sicilia ownership, representing the approachable tier of the sweet range.

milestones.event: First Oremus Aszú 5 puttonyos vintage

wines.description: Oremus's estate 5-puttonyos Aszú from Tolcsva; stone-fruit and marmalade concentration with Furmint's signature acidity.

verified.address_quoted: Bajcsy-Zsilinszky Endre utca 45., H-3934 Tolcsva, Hungary

wines.name: Oremus Tokaji Aszú 6 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in gönci oak barrels for 24 months

pairings.why: Aszú's sweetness and racy acidity provide the ideal counterpoint to foie gras richness.
Dried fig in the wine mirrors the jam; the cheese's salt sharpens the wine's sweetness.
Coffee and caramel in both wine and dessert form a complementary bridge.

history.summary: Oremus's 6-puttonyos Aszú is the flagship sweet wine of the estate, produced in exceptional years from the Tolcsva vineyards. The 13th-century cellars at Tolcsva provide an ideal environment for the slow oxidative maturation characteristic of this style.

milestones.event: First 6-puttonyos Aszú produced under Vega Sicilia ownership
Oremus establishes 6-puttonyos as the estate's top sweet-wine tier

wines.description: Oremus's flagship 6-puttonyos Aszú from the Tolcsva estate; deeply concentrated sweet wine aged in the estate's 13th-century cellars.

verified.address_quoted: Bajcsy-Zsilinszky Endre utca 45., H-3934 Tolcsva, Hungary

wines.name: Oremus Édes Szamorodni
wines.oak_regime: Aged in older oak; partial oxidative character

pairings.dish: Chicken liver spread on dark rye
pairings.why: The Szamorodni's sweetness and slight oxidative character match the liver's richness.
Hazelnut and baked apple notes in both the wine and cake form a natural pairing.
Nutty, salty hard cheese and the wine's hazelnut oxidative notes form a complementary match.

history.summary: Szamorodni édes is an ancient Tokaj style and Oremus's most traditional sweet wine, produced from whole-cluster harvesting of botrytised grapes alongside healthy bunches. The word Szamorodni derives from the Polish for 'as it was born' and describes the unsorted harvest approach.

milestones.event: First Oremus Édes Szamorodni produced commercially

wines.description: Oremus's traditional sweet Szamorodni from the Tolcsva estate, an amber-hued style with moderate sweetness and oxidative warmth.

verified.address_quoted: Bajcsy-Zsilinszky Endre utca 45., H-3934 Tolcsva, Hungary

wines.name: Oremus Eszencia
wines.oak_regime: Decades-long ageing in small oak; near-negligible alcohol

pairings.why: The concentration and complexity of Eszencia overwhelms most foods; contemplation is the primary pairing.
High-percentage chocolate is one of the rare counterparts that can stand alongside Eszencia's intensity.

history.summary: Oremus's Eszencia is produced in exceptional botrytis vintages only, from the gravity-drained free-run juice of hand-selected shrivelled Aszú berries. Historically used in the Habsburg court as a restorative, Oremus under Vega Sicilia began commercial production in the outstanding 1999 vintage.

milestones.event: Oremus first commercially releases Eszencia from the exceptional 1999 vintage
Another Eszencia produced from the highly botrytised 2007 harvest

wines.description: Oremus Eszencia: free-run Aszú-berry juice from exceptional vintages, with near-zero alcohol and extraordinary sugar concentration.

verified.address_quoted: Bajcsy-Zsilinszky Endre utca 45., H-3934 Tolcsva, Hungary

wines.name: Szepsy Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Aged in Hungarian oak barrels of various sizes

pairings.why: Mineral salinity and bright acidity pair with delicate raw fish preparations.
The wine's mineral body complements the sweet pepper and tomato of Hungary's vegetable stew.
Racy acidity cuts through creamy white sauces while complementing the delicate meat.

history.summary: István Szepsy is credited with pioneering the dry Furmint movement in Tokaj from the mid-1990s, demonstrating that the grape's extraordinary natural acidity and mineral character had a distinct identity beyond its role in sweet Aszú. The estate Furmint was his initial vehicle for this argument.

milestones.event: Szepsy begins commercial production of dry Furmint
Szepsy dry Furmints begin receiving international critical recognition

wines.description: Szepsy's estate dry Furmint from Mád; the wine that helped define dry Furmint as a serious category in Tokaj.

wines.name: Szepsy Úrágya Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Aged in Hungarian oak barrels

pairings.why: The wine's mineral and stone-fruit register complements luxurious crustacean preparations.
Salty, nutty hard cheese brings out the wine's mineral and tension.
Sweet shellfish and earthy celeriac find a complementary partner in the mineral Furmint.

history.summary: Úrágya ('master's bed') is one of Mád's important classified dűlők and one of Szepsy's flagship single-vineyard dry Furmint expressions, first vinified separately in 2002 as the estate deepened its single-site work.

milestones.event: First separate Úrágya single-vineyard vinification
Úrágya established as a top-tier Szepsy dry Furmint label

wines.description: Szepsy's single-vineyard Úrágya Furmint from Mád, a structured dry white with graphite mineral tension and long ageing potential.

wines.name: Szepsy Szent Tamás Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Aged in Hungarian oak barrels, 12-15 months

pairings.why: The wine's mineral clarity and high acidity cut through the paprika-rich soup broth.

pairings.dish: Roasted chicken with lemon and herbs
pairings.why: Lemon and herb notes in the wine mirror classic roasting aromatics.
Chalk mineral and tangy cheese form a natural textural and flavour match.

history.summary: The Szt. Tamás (St. Thomas) dűlő is one of Mád's three first-class classified vineyards. Szepsy began separate dry Furmint vinification from this site in 2003, paralleling the single-vineyard sweet-wine tradition with an equivalent dry-wine expression.

milestones.event: First separate Szt. Tamás Furmint dry vinification by Szepsy

wines.description: Szepsy's single-vineyard dry Furmint from the first-class Szt. Tamás dűlő in Mád.

wines.name: Szepsy Cuvée
wines.oak_regime: Aged in oak, blend from multiple Mád dűlők

pairings.why: The wine's citrus and mineral precision is a classic partner for simply prepared flatfish.
Mineral and herbal notes complement the earthiness of asparagus in a risotto context.
Hungary's national dish finds a natural regional partner in Mád's dry white blend.

history.summary: Szepsy's Cuvée was introduced to offer a multi-dűlő perspective on the estate's dry wine range, blending Furmint and Hárslevelű from different Mád classified sites for a more accessible and versatile expression than the individual single-vineyard bottlings.

milestones.event: Szepsy Cuvée introduced as a blended dry-wine tier

wines.description: Szepsy's multi-vineyard dry white Cuvée from Mád, blending Furmint and Hárslevelű from the estate's classified dűlők.

wines.name: Szepsy Tokaji Aszú 6 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Long maturation in gönci Hungarian oak for 24-36 months

pairings.why: The most traditional and ideal pairing for 6-puttonyos Aszú.
Crystalline umami and salt find a match in the wine's dried-fruit complexity.
Walnut and orange harmonise with the wine's tobacco-orange dried-fruit register.

history.summary: Szepsy's Aszú 6 puttonyos was among the first privately produced single-estate Aszú wines of the post-Communist era, emerging from the historic Mád dűlők that the Szepsy family had tended for generations. Produced only in genuine botrytis years, each vintage is a rare release.

milestones.event: First post-1989 Szepsy Aszú released commercially
1999 Szepsy Aszú gains international export distribution
Aszú regulations revised; Szepsy 6-puttonyos confirmed under new PDO rules

wines.description: Szepsy's Tokaji Aszú 6 puttonyos from Mád's classified dűlők; produced only in exceptional botrytis years with long oak maturation.

wines.name: Szepsy Tokaji Aszú 5 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in gönci oak for 18-24 months

pairings.why: The wine's dried-fruit and orange character complements duck and citrus preparations.
Sweetness and acidity handle the contrast of pungent blue cheese and sweet honey.
Cinnamon and apple notes resonate with the wine's spiced fruit character.

history.summary: Szepsy's 5-puttonyos Aszú is the more frequently produced tier of the estate's sweet wine, made when botrytis levels are significant but below the threshold for 6-puttonyos classification.

milestones.event: First Szepsy 5-puttonyos Aszú commercial release

wines.description: Szepsy's 5-puttonyos Aszú from Mád's dűlők; the accessible tier of the estate's iconic sweet-wine range.

wines.name: Holdvölgy Notabene Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged in Hungarian oak barrels

pairings.why: The wine's mineral acidity and citrus notes are a natural companion for fresh-water fish.
The savoury mineral register pairs well with charred vegetables.
Citrus and saline mineral in the wine echo tangy fresh cheese.

history.summary: Holdvölgy's Notabene Furmint was established as the estate's dry flagship from their Mád vineyards, produced from the old-vine parcels above the cellar tunnels of Mád's famous underground cave system, which runs nearly 2 km.

milestones.event: First Notabene Furmint from Holdvölgy's Mád vineyards

wines.description: Holdvölgy's flagship dry Furmint from Mád; mineral-driven and precise from the estate's volcanic-tuff classified plots.

wines.name: Holdvölgy Aszú 5 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in Hungarian oak for 18 months

pairings.why: Classic Aszú pairing: the sweet-acid tension counterbalances the richness of foie gras.
Dried apricot in the wine amplifies the stone-fruit character of the dessert.
Blue cheese salt and walnut find an echo in the wine's hazelnut-caramel notes.

history.summary: Holdvölgy first produced Aszú in the outstanding 2007 vintage, using their holdings in Mád's classified dűlők. The estate's old underground cellars provide ideal stable conditions for Aszú maturation.

milestones.event: First Holdvölgy Aszú from the outstanding 2007 botrytis vintage

wines.description: Holdvölgy's 5-puttonyos Aszú from the Mád estate; sweet and layered with dried stone fruit and caramelised citrus.

wines.name: Holdvölgy Vision Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large-format older Hungarian oak

pairings.why: The wine's fresh citrus and light mineral character complements herb-accented freshwater fish.
Hungary's classic cucumber salad finds a natural regional partner in a fresh Furmint.
Light, fresh cheese and the wine's clean mineral character work together.

history.summary: Vision was introduced as Holdvölgy's approachable everyday dry Furmint label, positioned below Notabene to offer a fresh and accessible entry to the estate's dry-wine philosophy.

milestones.event: Vision Furmint introduced as the estate-entry dry-wine label

wines.description: Holdvölgy's fresh entry-level dry Furmint, approachable and mineral-driven for everyday enjoyment.

wines.name: Holdvölgy Eloquence Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Barrel-fermented in new and older Hungarian oak, extended lees contact

pairings.why: The wine's full body and nutty barrel notes complement rich cream sauces on white meat.
Nutty hard cheeses echo the wine's barrel-fermentation character.
The full body and cream notes pair well with rich shellfish soups.

history.summary: Eloquence was conceived as Holdvölgy's premium dry Furmint tier, demonstrating the grape's capacity to take barrel fermentation and extended lees contact while retaining its mineral identity.

milestones.event: Eloquence introduced as the barrel-fermented premium dry Furmint label

wines.description: Holdvölgy's barrel-fermented premium dry Furmint; fuller and creamier than the Notabene, with nutty depth and mineral backbone.

wines.name: Patricius Dry Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel fermentation, no oak

pairings.why: The wine's clean acidity and white pepper cut through fried coatings.
Fresh mineral and citrus complement the brightness of light spring vegetable soups.
The wine's clean mineral character pairs simply with fresh dairy.

history.summary: Patricius's dry Furmint was introduced as the estate's accessible everyday wine from their large Bodrogkisfalud holdings, representing the cleanest and most straightforward expression of the variety.

milestones.event: First dry Furmint released by Patricius

wines.description: Patricius Winery's everyday dry Furmint from the 68-hectare Bodrogkisfalud estate; clean and mineral with a peppery citrus character.

wines.name: Patricius Aszú 6 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in oak barrels for 18-24 months

pairings.why: Aszú's sweet-acid character is a natural match for rich liver preparations.
Dried fig in the wine mirrors the garnish; blue cheese salt is tamed by sweetness.
Dried-fruit character in both the wine and a classic fruit cake create a complementary pairing.

history.summary: Patricius began Aszú production in the outstanding 2003 vintage, using the estate's significant holdings at Bodrogkisfalud, a village noted for its reliable autumn fog conditions that promote botrytis formation.

milestones.event: First Patricius Aszú 6 puttonyos from the highly botrytised 2003 vintage

wines.description: Patricius Winery's 6-puttonyos Aszú from Bodrogkisfalud; concentrated dried-fruit sweetness with the house's characteristic high acidity.

wines.name: Patricius Édes Szamorodni
wines.oak_regime: Matured in gönci oak for 12 months

pairings.why: Walnut in both the wine and Hungary's traditional Christmas pastry form a natural match.
Caramelised apple and cinnamon echo the wine's spiced fruit character.
Light sweetness and moderate concentration suit milder blue cheeses.

history.summary: Patricius's Édes Szamorodni uses the estate's fog-prone Bodrogkisfalud vineyards where reliable autumn mists produce well-botrytised grapes suitable for the traditional whole-cluster Szamorodni harvest.

milestones.event: Patricius Édes Szamorodni first produced

wines.description: Patricius's traditional sweet Szamorodni from Bodrogkisfalud; honey and walnut with moderate sweetness and oxidative warmth.

wines.name: Patricius Hárslevelű
wines.oak_regime: Partial oak maturation

pairings.dish: Poached white fish in herb broth
pairings.why: Delicate floral and citrus notes complement light fish preparations.
Floral and herbal notes in the wine match asparagus's vegetal character.
Lime and herb aromas align with lemon-herb chicken preparations.

history.summary: Patricius cultivates Hárslevelű as a varietal wine alongside Furmint, taking advantage of their 68-hectare estate which includes the variety. Hárslevelű means 'linden leaf' in Hungarian and is prized for its floral aromatics.

milestones.event: First varietal Hárslevelű from Patricius

wines.description: Patricius's varietal Hárslevelű; floral, lime-scented and light-bodied, showcasing this less-bottled Tokaj variety.

wines.name: Patricius Sárga Muskotály
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel, no oak

history.summary: The Lőcse vineyard at the Bénye Basin was identified by Béres as the flagship of their 45-hectare estate's plots, producing Furmint with distinctly volcanic rhyolite-tuff minerality from the geographical heart of the Tokaj appellation.

milestones.event: First Lőcse single-vineyard Furmint from Béres

wines.description: Béres Vineyards' entry-level dry Furmint; fresh, crisp and mineral from the Bénye Basin's volcanic soils.

wines.name: Kikelet Furmint

milestones.event: Kikelet Pince founded by Stéphanie and Zsolt Berecz in Tarcal
First Kikelet dry Furmint vintage
Stéphanie Berecz awarded Winemaker of Winemakers by Hungarian wine peers

Lime and herb character echoes the risotto's bright aromatics.
Light, floral wine pairs naturally with soft citrus-accented cheese.

history.summary: Kikelet has cultivated Hárslevelű as a varietal since 2006, expressing the grape's distinctive linden-blossom aromatics through Stéphanie Berecz's mineral-focused vinification approach.

milestones.event: First full Kikelet Aszú 5 puttonyos vintage

wines.description: Kikelet Pince's 5-puttonyos Aszú from Tarcal; precise noble-rot expression with the estate's characteristic mineral acidity.

wines.name: Barta Öreg Király Dűlő Furmint

history.summary: Barta Winery first produced 6-puttonyos Aszú in the exceptional 2007 botrytis vintage, drawing on their parcels in Mád's classified dűlők above the Rákóczi utca winery.

milestones.event: First Barta Aszú 6 puttonyos from the outstanding 2007 vintage

wines.description: Barta Winery's 6-puttonyos Aszú from Mád's volcanic dűlők; concentrated and mineral-driven.

history.summary: Barta added Hárslevelű to their range in 2009 to give a fuller picture of Mád's traditional varieties alongside their Furmint, the two grapes historically grown side by side on the hillside dűlők.

Fresh mineral white with herby soft cheese is a classic low-effort pairing.

history.summary: Sauska launched their Tokaj label in 2012, combining the family's Villány-based experience with new plantings and acquisitions in the Rátka area of Tokaj. The Furmint entry is the estate's most accessible expression.

milestones.event: Sauska Tokaj first vintage of dry Furmint
New Rátka winery facility opened, improving production capacity

wines.description: Sauska Tokaj's entry-level dry Furmint from the Rátka estate; fresh, clean and mineral.

history.summary: Sauska's Aszú production began in 2013, drawing on their Rátka-area Tokaj holdings. The Sauska family's background in premium Hungarian winemaking from Villány provided the technical base for serious Aszú production.

wines.description: Sauska Tokaj's 6-puttonyos Aszú from the Rátka estate; classic style with concentrated dried fruit and vivid acidity.

history.summary: Sauska introduced their Tokaj Pezsgő (traditional-method sparkling wine) from Furmint as one of the few producers exploring the grape's sparkling potential. Furmint's naturally high acidity makes it structurally ideal for traditional-method sparkling production.

The Barakonyi single-vineyard Furmint was her first signature dry wine, expressing the pronounced saline mineral character of the Bodrogkeresztúr terroir that she has championed throughout her career.

First commercially marketed Pendits Furmint

First Csontos single-vineyard Furmint vinified separately

As one of the first family-run cellars to revive private Aszú production after the state-farm era, the estate holds historical significance for the region's revival.

milestones.event: Hímesudvar established on the historic 16th-century Bem Street site
First commercial dry Furmint production

pairings.why: Clean mineral Furmint with simply fried fish is a common pairing in the Tokaj region.

Clean, fresh dry Furmint as an aperitif is a standard throughout northeastern Hungary.

history.summary: Grand Tokaj's dry Furmint label was introduced around 2000 as the enterprise began responding to growing international interest in dry expressions of the grape, pivoting from its traditional sweet-wine focus.

wines.name: Demeter Zoltán Aszú 6 Puttonyos

pairings.why: Crystalline umami and salt match the wine's mineral intensity; honey bridges the sweetness.
Saffron honey notes in the wine resonate with a saffron-accented foie gras preparation.
Walnut-caramel notes in wine and dessert form a complementary echo.

history.summary: Demeter Zoltán's Aszú is produced only in exceptional botrytis years, from tiny Mád dűlő parcels. The wine has attracted significant cult following among specialists in Germany and Japan, though production quantities are minimal.

milestones.event: First Demeter Zoltán Aszú 6 puttonyos from the outstanding 2007 vintage

wines.description: Demeter Zoltán's boutique Aszú 6 puttonyos from Mád; mineral precision from tiny Mád dűlő parcels, produced in exceptional botrytis years only.

wines.name: Zsirai Mézes Mály Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Aged in Hungarian oak barrels

pairings.dish: Roast veal with lemon and thyme
pairings.why: The wine's full body and stone-fruit richness pair well with herb-roasted white meat.
Nutty hard cheese and the wine's full-bodied mineral character form a satisfying match.
Full-bodied dry Furmint complements rich cream-based vegetable soups.

history.summary: Zsirai Pince farms the southern slopes of Kopasz-hegy in Tarcal, including plots adjacent to the historic Mézes Mály first-class dűlő. Tarcal's southernmost position in Tokaj produces noticeably richer fruit than the northern villages, and Zsirai's proximity to Mézes Mály gives their dry Furmint a distinctive stone-fruit richness.

milestones.event: First Zsirai Mézes Mály single-vineyard Furmint

wines.description: Zsirai Pince's Mézes Mály Furmint from Tarcal's south-facing slopes; full-bodied and rich from the warmest village in Tokaj.

wines.name: Zsirai Aszú 6 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in gönci oak for 18-24 months

pairings.why: The rich fruit concentration of a Tarcal Aszú pairs excellently with foie gras and apricot.
Rich fruit and chocolate are a classic pairing for concentrated Aszú.
The wine's richness and sweetness handle even the most pungent blue cheese.

history.summary: Zsirai Pince first produced 6-puttonyos Aszú in the outstanding 2007 botrytis vintage. The Tarcal terroir's south-facing maximum exposure produces noticeably richer botrytised berries than the cooler northern villages.

milestones.event: First Zsirai Aszú 6 puttonyos from the exceptional 2007 vintage

wines.description: Zsirai Pince's 6-puttonyos Aszú from Tarcal; rich and opulent from the most south-facing slopes in Tokaj.

wines.name: Balassa Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Older oak or stainless steel; traditional Tolcsva cellar ageing

pairings.why: Mineral Furmint is the classic Hungarian pairing for the region's freshwater fish.
Clean citrus and mineral complements lightly fried seasonal vegetables.
Mineral dry Furmint with fresh local cheeses is the everyday Tokaj combination.

history.summary: Balassa Pince is based in Tolcsva, the same village as Oremus, and uses the deep historic cellar network carved through volcanic tuff for ageing. The estate produces dry Furmint and traditional Szamorodni from the Tolcsva hillsides.

milestones.event: First commercial Balassa dry Furmint

wines.description: Balassa Pince's dry Furmint from Tolcsva; mineral and citrus-driven, aged in the village's historic volcanic-tuff cellar tunnels.

wines.name: Balassa Szamorodni Édes
wines.oak_regime: Aged in gönci oak for 12-18 months with partial oxidative character

pairings.why: Caramelised apple and cinnamon in the wine mirror the flavours of this classic cake.
Traditional Hungarian honey-walnut roll and sweet Szamorodni are a natural regional pairing.
Moderate sweetness and hazelnut character complement aged cheeses that aren't too strong.

history.summary: Balassa produces Szamorodni as the traditional sweet expression from the Tolcsva historic cellars, reflecting the ancient style that predates modern Aszú classification by centuries.

milestones.event: First commercial Balassa Szamorodni édes

wines.description: Balassa Pince's sweet Szamorodni from Tolcsva's historic cellar tunnels; traditional amber-hued style with honey apple and oxidative warmth.

wines.name: Kovács Nimród Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel or older oak

pairings.why: Clean mineral Furmint works with Hungary's paprika-based fish dishes.
Mineral and citrus pair with fresh tangy cheese and herb accents.
The wine's crisp acidity and clean mineral complement a cool cucumber-yoghurt starter.

history.summary: Winemaker Nimród Kovács produces wines from Mezőzombor, the same village as Disznókő's large estate, but at a much smaller boutique scale. The estate's range includes the rare Kabar variety alongside the dominant Furmint.

milestones.event: First commercial Kovács Nimród dry Furmint

wines.description: Kovács Nimród Winery's dry Furmint from Mezőzombor; clean and mineral from the same village as Disznókő's large estate.

wines.name: Kovács Nimród Aszú 5 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in gönci oak for 18 months

pairings.why: Sweet-acid balance of 5-puttonyos Aszú is a traditional match for goose and duck liver.
Ginger spice in the wine echoes the baking spice of gingerbread.
Moderate sweetness and acidity handle mild blue cheeses effectively.

history.summary: Kovács Nimród Winery first produced Aszú from their Mezőzombor holdings in 2009, drawing on the botrytis conditions of the fog-prone Bodrog valley below the estate.

milestones.event: First Kovács Nimród Aszú 5 puttonyos

wines.description: Kovács Nimród Winery's 5-puttonyos Aszú from Mezőzombor; boutique expression of the classic style with spiced citrus character.

wines.name: Tokaj Hétszőlős Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Aged in older Hungarian oak barrels

pairings.why: Mineral Furmint is the local complement for the region's freshwater trout.
Delicate raw meat and fresh herb dressing find a partner in the wine's mineral acidity.
Tangy fresh cheese and mineral dry Furmint are a natural combination.

history.summary: Tokaj Hétszőlős takes its name from the famous seven-vineyard ('Hét szőlő') classification concept in Mád. The estate produces Furmint and Aszú from their Mád vineyards.

milestones.event: First commercial Hétszőlős dry Furmint

wines.description: Tokaj Hétszőlős's dry Furmint from Mád; mineral and precise from the volcanic-tuff classified zone.

wines.name: Tokaj Hétszőlős Aszú 5 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in gönci oak for 18 months

pairings.why: Sweet-acid balance of Aszú complements rich duck liver; quince echoes the wine's fruit.
Stone fruit and almond in the tart echo the wine's apricot-chamomile notes.
The Aszú's sweetness and acidity balance strong mature cheddar and dried figs.

history.summary: Tokaj Hétszőlős first produced 5-puttonyos Aszú in 2008 from their Mád classified dűlő holdings.

milestones.event: First Hétszőlős Aszú 5 puttonyos from Mád

wines.description: Tokaj Hétszőlős's 5-puttonyos Aszú from Mád's classified dűlők; dried stone fruit with chamomile and high Furmint acidity.

wines.name: Szepsy Jr. Úrágya Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Aged in Hungarian oak barrels of various sizes

pairings.why: Mineral salinity and stone-fruit tension pair with luxurious shellfish preparations.

pairings.dish: Poached sole with herb beurre blanc
pairings.why: The wine's mineral-citrus precision is a classic match for delicate sole.
Nutty-salty hard cheese and the wine's mineral tension are a natural combination.

history.summary: István Szepsy Jr. is the son of the dry Furmint pioneer István Szepsy and has established his own separate boutique label, working parcels in Mád's classified dűlők including Úrágya. The younger Szepsy's wines began attracting specialist attention from the 2016 vintage.

milestones.event: First Szepsy Jr. separate label production from Mád dűlők

wines.description: Szepsy Jr.'s own-label single-vineyard Furmint from the Úrágya dűlő in Mád; the next generation's interpretation of Mád mineral precision.

wines.name: Szepsy Jr. Nyulászó Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Aged in Hungarian oak barrels

pairings.why: Mineral salinity and citrus tension pair with freshwater crayfish.
Almond finish in the wine complements an almond-crusted poultry preparation.
Citrus precision and acidity cut through the milky richness of fresh mozzarella.

history.summary: Szepsy Jr. began separate vinification of the Nyulászó dűlő in 2017, adding a second major Mád first-class single-vineyard expression to his own label and creating a generational parallel with his father's Aszú work from the same site.

milestones.event: First Szepsy Jr. Nyulászó Furmint from the Mád first-class dűlő

wines.description: Szepsy Jr.'s single-vineyard dry Furmint from the Nyulászó first-class dűlő in Mád; floral-lemon and saline character from this celebrated site.

wines.name: Szepsy Jr. Aszú 6 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Long maturation in gönci oak; produced in excellent botrytis years only

pairings.dish: Foie gras with mineral sea salt
pairings.why: The Aszú's mineral-sweet character is a natural counterpoint to sea-salt-accented foie gras.
Crystalline umami and chestnut honey provide an unusual but complementary match.
Mineral-sweet tension in the wine pairs with salt-flaked dark chocolate.

history.summary: Szepsy Jr. first produced Aszú in the excellent 2018 botrytis vintage, applying his characteristic mineral-precision approach to the sweet style and continuing the family tradition of Mád single-vineyard Aszú production.

milestones.event: First Szepsy Jr. Aszú 6 puttonyos from exceptional botrytis year

wines.description: Szepsy Jr.'s Aszú 6 puttonyos from Mád's classified dűlők; mineral-precision sweet wine from the next-generation Szepsy cellar.

wines.name: Malatinszky Tokaj Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Older oak or stainless steel

pairings.why: Mineral acidity and citrus are a classic complement for grilled freshwater fish.
The wine's clean mineral freshness works with Hungary's traditional cream-finished soups.

pairings.dish: Fresh cream cheese on dark rye
pairings.why: Crisp mineral Furmint with fresh dairy on dark rye bread is the everyday Tokaj pairing.

history.summary: The Malatinszky family, known primarily for their Villány red wines, extended their label to Tokaj in 2014, producing dry Furmint and Aszú from the region's classified vineyards.

milestones.event: Malatinszky Tokaj label launched with first dry Furmint vintage

wines.description: Malatinszky Tokaj's dry Furmint; mineral and citrus-driven from a Villány-family's extension into the Tokaj white-wine appellation.

wines.name: Malatinszky Tokaji Aszú 5 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Aged in gönci oak for 18 months

pairings.why: Classic Aszú pairing; sweet-acid balance complements rich duck liver.
Soft cheese warmth and apricot garnish echo the wine's stone-fruit character.
Stone fruit and cream are natural partners for 5-puttonyos Aszú.

history.summary: Malatinszky produced their first Tokaj Aszú in 2016, two years after establishing the dry Furmint label, completing their Tokaj range with the region's traditional sweet expression.

milestones.event: First Malatinszky Tokaji Aszú 5 puttonyos

wines.description: Malatinszky Tokaji's 5-puttonyos Aszú; clean and well-balanced from the Villány family's Tokaj extension.

The wine's high acidity and sweetness handle a pungent gorgonzola with honey.
Quince and almond in the tart echo the wine's quince-nut character.

history.summary: The Birsalmás dűlő (named for the quince trees once bordering the vineyard) was added to Royal Tokaji's single-vineyard Aszú lineup in 1999, completing the five-vineyard single-site program of the estate.

milestones.event: First Birsalmás single-vineyard Aszú 6 puttonyos from Royal Tokaji

wines.description: Royal Tokaji's single-vineyard 6-puttonyos Aszú from the Birsalmás dűlő; quince-tinged fruit with characteristic mineral backbone.

wines.name: Royal Tokaji Dry Furmint Estate
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; minimal oak contact

pairings.why: Clean mineral Furmint with simply prepared trout is the everyday Mád pairing.
The wine's clean mineral acidity cuts through tahini-based dips.
Crisp, fresh dry Furmint as an aperitif suits warm weather and casual occasions.

history.summary: Royal Tokaji's Estate Dry Furmint was introduced as the accessible entry point to the estate's dry white range, positioned below The Oddity and intended for everyday drinking rather than cellaring.

milestones.event: Royal Tokaji Dry Furmint Estate introduced as the entry dry-wine label

wines.description: Royal Tokaji's entry-level dry Furmint Estate; fresh, clean and mineral, an accessible introduction to the estate's dry wine range.

wines.name: Disznókő Száraz Szamorodni

Nutty aged hard cheese and the wine's walnut-oxidative notes are a natural match.
Earthy, oxidative dry Szamorodni complements mushroom and walnut preparations.

history.summary: Száraz Szamorodni is Tokaj's rarest and most underappreciated style, dry and oxidative from whole-cluster mixed-harvest fermentation. Disznókő produces it in tiny quantities, offering a Tokaj dry wine with a completely different character from modern mineral dry Furmint.

milestones.event: Disznókő Száraz Szamorodni first produced commercially

wines.description: Disznókő's dry Szamorodni; an oxidative, nutty style from whole-cluster mixed-harvest fermentation, Tokaj's rarest dry-wine category.

wines.name: Oremus Petracs Furmint

Mineral and stone-fruit precision pairs with asparagus and rich sauce.
The wine's mineral depth and almond character complement truffle pasta.

history.summary: Oremus introduced the Petracs single-vineyard label in 2005 as a second named-site Furmint alongside Mandolás, expressing a different facet of the Tolcsva valley's volcanic terroir.

milestones.event: First Oremus Petracs single-vineyard Furmint bottled separately

wines.description: Oremus's Petracs single-vineyard dry Furmint from Tolcsva; almond-mineral character from this secondary named-site label.

Honey notes in the wine and garnish bridge to mild blue cheese.
Fresh apricot in the wine mirrors the soufflé's fruit character.

history.summary: Patricius added a 5-puttonyos tier to their Aszú range in 2005, two years after the 6-puttonyos debut, offering a slightly less concentrated but more frequently produced sweet wine.

wines.description: Patricius Winery's 5-puttonyos Aszú from the 68-hectare Bodrogkisfalud estate; fresh apricot and honeyed citrus with characteristic high acidity.

milestones.event: First Erzsébet Pince Édes Szamorodni

wines.description: Erzsébet Pince's traditional sweet Szamorodni from Tokaj town; honey-apple and nutty warmth from the historic Bem Street cellar.

The wine's acidity balances the cream-enriched paprika sauce.
Crisp dry Furmint and tangy fresh cheese share complementary mineral tones.

history.summary: Family estate in Hercegkút, a Swabian-settled village known for its barrel-vaulted cellar street built into the hillside. The dry Furmint reflects the cooler mesoclimate of this northwestern corner of the Tokaj PDO.

milestones.event: Hárslevelű first bottled as a varietal wine at Almási Pince

history.summary: Five-puttonyos Aszú represents the traditional mid-range of Tokaj PDO sweet wine, made by macerating hand-selected aszú berries in must or wine. The 5 Puttonyos designation requires a minimum of 120 g/L residual sugar. The 2013 reform removed the 3 and 4 puttonyos categories.

milestones.event: Post-2013 PDO reform: only 5 and 6 Puttonyos designations remain; Almási first certified bottling under the new rules

wines.description: Tokaji Aszú 5 Puttonyos from Almási Pince in Hercegkút; traditional botrytised sweet wine from volcanic soils with high acidity and concentrated stone-fruit character.

wines.name: Vincze Pince Furmint

High-acid Furmint cuts through the rich butter sauce.
The wine's minerality complements tangy fresh dairy.

history.summary: Family estate in Erdőbénye, a hillside village at higher elevation than the valley-floor wine towns. The forested ridges moderate temperatures and extend ripening, producing dry Furmint with pronounced natural acidity.

milestones.event: Vincze Pince first bottled varietal Furmint under estate label

wines.oak_regime: Gönci barrel maturation in underground volcanic tuff cellar

Botrytis wine and custard desserts share vanilla and caramel tones.
Sweet wine and rich liver pâté are a traditional Hungarian pairing.

wines.name: Fekete Pince Furmint

Crisp acidity echoes the cooling freshness of the classic Hungarian side dish.
The wine's acidity and clean finish complement the lean white meat.

milestones.event: Fekete Pince established estate bottling program in Sárospatak

wines.description: Dry Furmint from Fekete Pince in Sárospatak; northern-sector Tokaj with firm high acidity and clean mineral character.

The wine's gentle body complements the fried dough and creamy topping.
Light sweet-savoury cheese filling harmonises with the floral wine aromatics.

history.summary: Hárslevelű (linden-leaf) is the second-most planted variety in Tokaj PDO, valued for its floral aromatics and contribution to the Aszú blend. Fekete Pince bottles it as a varietal alongside Furmint.

milestones.event: First varietal Hárslevelű bottled by Fekete Pince

wines.description: Dry varietal Hárslevelű from Fekete Pince in Sárospatak; floral linden-blossom aromatics and gentle medium body.

wines.name: Fekete Pince Tokaji Aszú 5 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Gönci barrel maturation in cool underground cellar

pairings.why: Noble-rot sweetness and high acidity are the classic foil for strong blue cheese.
The Aszú's fruit-forward sweetness echoes the tart cherry in this traditional dessert.
The wine's sweetness and acidity cut through smoky richness.

history.summary: Aszú production in northern Sárospatak benefits from the town's historic wine-cellar culture; the post-2013 PDO reform standardised Aszú at 5 and 6 Puttonyos only.

milestones.event: PDO reform finalised; Fekete Pince certified first 5 Puttonyos under new rules

wines.description: Tokaji Aszú 5 Puttonyos from Fekete Pince in northern Sárospatak; botrytised sweetness with firm acidity from the cooler northern sector.

wines.name: Harsányi Pince Furmint

pairings.why: The wine's crisp acidity refreshes the palate between sips of the paprika-heavy soup.
High-acid Furmint cuts through the fat in smoked fish preparations.
The wine's acidity harmonises with the fermented tartness of sauerkraut.

history.summary: Harsányi Pince in Sátoraljaújhely at the northern tip of the appellation bordering Slovakia. The Zemplén foothills shelter south-facing terraces that allow Furmint to ripen despite the northerly position; wines show a more austerely mineral character than those from the warmer central Mád basin.

milestones.event: Harsányi Pince established estate bottling of dry Furmint

wines.description: Dry Furmint from Harsányi Pince in Sátoraljaújhely, the northernmost Tokaj village. Austere, high-acid mineral style from the appellation's coolest zone.

wines.name: Harsányi Pince Tokaji Aszú 5 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Traditional Gönci barrel ageing in underground cellar

pairings.why: Classic pairing; the wine's sweetness and acidity balance the salt and fat of blue cheese.
The wine's stone-fruit character and sweetness harmonise with the pastry filling.
Rich liver fat is balanced by the Aszú's acidity and sweet fruit.

history.summary: Aszú production in Sátoraljaújhely requires north-tilted south-facing terraces; botrytis develops reliably in the autumn mists off the Ronyva stream. The post-2013 PDO reform restricted Aszú to 5 and 6 Puttonyos designations.

milestones.event: First Harsányi Pince 5 Puttonyos Aszú certified post-2013 PDO reform

wines.description: Tokaji Aszú 5 Puttonyos from Harsányi Pince in Sátoraljaújhely; firm acidity and botrytised richness from the northernmost corner of the appellation.

wines.name: Katona Pince Furmint
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel

High-acid Furmint cuts through the fried coating and rich river fish fat.
The wine's fresh minerality balances the salt and fat of Hungarian sheep's cheese.

history.summary: Katona Pince in Abaújszántó occupies the southernmost wine village of the Tokaj-Hegyalja PDO; warmer average temperatures produce wines with riper fruit profiles. The village is less visited than Mád or Tokaj town but holds old vine stocks.

milestones.event: Katona Pince begins estate-bottling under family label in Abaújszántó

wines.description: Dry Furmint from Katona Pince in Abaújszántó, the southernmost village of the Tokaj PDO; riper fruit character from warmer south-facing volcanic terraces.

wines.name: Katona Pince Sárga Muskotály

Light floral wine refreshes the palate between bites of fruit-based dessert.
Aromatic Muscat and light-textured fresh dairy are a delicate pairing.

history.summary: Sárga Muskotály (Yellow Muscat) is one of the minor approved varieties of the Tokaj PDO, grown mainly in warmer southern villages. Katona Pince bottles it as a varietal, a relatively rare expression in the region.

milestones.event: Katona Pince first varietal Sárga Muskotály bottling

wines.description: Varietal dry Sárga Muskotály from Katona Pince in Abaújszántó; intensely floral Muscat aromatics on a light, refreshing dry frame.

wines.name: Katona Pince Tokaji Aszú 5 Puttonyos
wines.oak_regime: Gönci barrel ageing in underground cellar

pairings.why: The wine's floral Muscat notes and sweetness echo warm soft cheese with honeycomb.
Aromatic Muscat in the blend harmonises with almond-based confectionery.
